message:
fix(fp-columnar): Column.combine honors fill_value=None null propagation (frankenpandas-x0b5)

pandas pd.Series.combine(other, func, fill_value=None) propagates
null through func without invoking it when fill_value is None —
missing-on-either-side positions should emit Null(NaN) without a
user-function call. The previous Column::combine required a Scalar
fill, effectively always acting as if fill_value was non-None and
invoking func with the fill value substituted for any missing input.

Signature change: fill: Scalar → fill: Option<Scalar>. Callers pass
Some(v) to preserve the old "substitute-then-invoke" behavior and
None for pandas-default null propagation. The existing test that
passed Scalar::Int64(0) is migrated to Some(Scalar::Int64(0)).

Two new tests cover the None path:
- combine_fill_none_propagates_nulls_without_invoking_func tracks a
  mutable call counter to prove func is NOT called for null
  positions.
- combine_fill_none_all_present_matches_elementwise_apply confirms
  the non-null path is an elementwise apply when both sides are
  populated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
